items.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
items.description: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
items.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

items.title: Trump’s pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
items.description: Sean Plankey has requested to withdraw his name to run the U.S. cybersecurity agency after a tumultuous year of chaotic temporary leadership.

items.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
items.description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

items.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
items.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

items.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs, or 8,000 employees, report says
items.description: According to an internal memo, Meta plans to begin its sweeping layoffs on May 20.

items.title: Bluesky now supports better-quality photos
items.description: The max size is now 2MB and max resolution is 4000 x 4000.

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.description: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
items.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

items.title: Meta is revamping its cross-app management system
items.description: Meta's Accounts Center will transition to the Meta Account, which is supposed to make it easier to manage your experience on various Meta apps and devices.

items.title: X is shutting down Communities because of low usage and lots of spam
items.description: X Communities are no more. Only a fraction of X users were using Communities, and much of that use was spam, the company said.

items.title: Salmon raises $100M in equity and debt to bring digital credit to underbanked Filipinos
items.description: Salmon is targeting underbanked Filipinos, with little to no credit history, as well as people unhappy with the incumbent lenders' reliability issues.

items.title: Vercel says some of its customers’ data was stolen prior to its recent hack
items.description: The app and website hosting company has found evidence of a second compromise of customer accounts after expanding its initial investigation following a breach in early April.

items.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
items.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

items.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
items.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

items.title: Labor unrest at Samsung may worsen memory chip supply issues
items.description: Tens of thousands of Samsung Electronics workers held a rally at the company’s Pyeongtaek campus in South Korea on Thursday to signal they are prepared to walk off the job for an 18-day strike next month.

items.title: Tesla drops Musk’s $29B ‘interim’ award after Delaware court restored larger pay package
items.description: The company is following through on its promise to not let Musk "double dip" if he won his appeal to restore Tesla's 2018 compensation package, worth $56 billion.

items.title: WhatsApp adds prepaid phone recharges in India as its payments usage still lags
items.description: WhatsApp is rolling out prepaid phone recharges to its 500 million users in India via PayU.

items.title: AI galaxy hunters are adding to the global GPU crunch
items.description: Astronomers are turning to GPUs to find needles in the galactic haystack.

items.title: Surveillance vendors caught abusing access to telcos to track people’s phone locations, researchers say
items.description: The Citizen Lab found two separate surveillance vendors abusing the backbone of cellular networks to spy on several victims across the world.
